title: Longest Nice Subarray
description:
You are given an array nums consisting of positive integers.

We call a subarray of nums nice if the bitwise AND of every pair of elements that are in different positions in the subarray is equal to 0.

Return _the length of the longest nice subarray_.

A subarray is a contiguous part of an array.

Note that subarrays of length 1 are always considered nice.

solutionCode.python:
```python
def longestNiceSubarray(nums):
    n = len(nums)
    max_len = 1
    for i in range(n):
        mask = 0
        for j in range(i, n):
            mask |= nums[j]
            if mask & (mask - 1) == 0:
                max_len = max(max_len, j - i + 1)
    return max_len
```
    
    The algorithm iterates through all possible subarrays of the input array. For each subarray, a bitwise-OR mask is maintained, such that its bits represent the union of bits in all elements of the subarray. We check if the mask is a power of two (i.e., if there is only one bit set to 1). If the mask is a power of two, that means all bits in every pair of elements in the subarray do not overlap and hence their pairwise bitwise-AND is zero, satisfying the nice subarray condition. In this case, we update the maximum length of the nice subarray.

The time complexity of the algorithm is O(n^2) since we iterate through all possible subarrays. The space complexity is O(1) as we only maintain a mask and max_len.
